methodsAttempted.query: site:mq.edu.au Macquarie University artificial intelligence policy generative AI students assessment academic integrity
methodsAttempted.notes: Found official student, library, policy, and TECHE results; selected official policy and student/library guidance for fetch.

methodsAttempted.query: site:policies.mq.edu.au Macquarie University artificial intelligence policy assessment generative AI Academic Integrity Policy
methodsAttempted.notes: Found Responsible and Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence Policy and Academic Integrity Policy in Policy Central.
Followed official student/library guidance links to referencing guidance and research guidance; SharePoint research page required login and was rejected.
Fetched official Guidance Note on Using Generative Artificial Intelligence in Research PDF.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found Macquarie Policy Central AI and academic-integrity policy pages, student-facing AI assessment guidance, Waranara Library AI and referencing guidance, and the official research guidance PDF. TECHE advisory content and login-gated SharePoint content were rejected for policy evidence.

artifacts.claimText: Macquarie University's Responsible and Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence Policy applies to staff, students, and affiliates across all uses of AI and sets eight principles for responsible and ethical AI use.

citation.citationTitle: Responsible and Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence Policy
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Responsible and Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence Policy
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: This Policy outlines the principles that support the responsible and ethical use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) at Macquarie University. This Policy applies to all Staff, Students and Affiliates of the University, encompassing all uses of Artificial Intelligence (AI) by these groups.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The policy is university-wide for staff, students, and affiliates and covers all AI uses under responsible and ethical AI principles.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Macquarie's AI policy says University-provided AI tools and systems are subject to privacy, security, intellectual-property, intended-use, and risk-assessment controls.

citation.citationTitle: Responsible and Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence Policy
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Responsible and Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence Policy
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The University will undertake regular assessments to ensure that the use of AI systems and tools does not compromise data security or intellectual property rights and complies with University policy. Users of AI tools and systems provided by the University must use these for their intended purpose... A risk assessment will be conducted by the Head of AI prior to the implementation of an AI system.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Macquarie links AI systems to security/IP checks, intended-use requirements, and risk assessment before implementation.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Section 2 - Privacy and Security; Human Oversight and Responsibility
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Macquarie student guidance categorises assessment tasks as Open or Observed: Open tasks allow generative AI with acknowledgement and student responsibility, while Observed tasks may restrict or prohibit AI use.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Your assessment tasks will be categorised as either Open or Observed. Open assessments do not restrict the application of AI tools. However, it is expected that the use of AI is acknowledged in an appropriate manner... Observed tasks are either fully or partly observed or invigilated. AI use will either not be permitted or might be restricted in a particular way during the assessment.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Assessment AI permissions are task-specific: Open allows AI with acknowledgement; Observed may restrict or prohibit it.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Macquarie's approach to AI and assessment
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Macquarie student assessment guidance says Unit Guides are the definitive source for whether a task is Open or Observed, with iLearn providing more detailed assessment information.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Unit Guides are your definitive source for assessment information. Your unit guide will show you whether a task is Open or Observed. iLearn is typically where you'll find more comprehensive information about your assessments.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students should use Unit Guides and iLearn to confirm assessment-specific AI permissions.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Where to find information about the use of AI tools

artifacts.claimText: Macquarie's Academic Integrity Policy treats unauthorised use of generative AI in an academic exercise as unacceptable academic conduct that may lead to an academic integrity breach allegation.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Unacceptable Academic Conduct may lead to an allegation of an academic integrity breach... Unauthorised use of generative artificial intelligence occurs when a student uses material produced by a generative artificial intelligence in an academic exercise, without authorisation and submits it as their own work.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Unauthorised GenAI use is explicitly listed as unacceptable academic conduct in the Academic Integrity Policy.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Macquarie Library student guidance lists several MQ-accessible AI-enabled tools and tells students in AI-Open assessments to check AI output carefully and acknowledge AI use.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Macquarie provides a suite of AI-enabled tools to support your learning... MQ Virtual Peer... MultiSearch Research Assistant... Web of Science Research Assistant... Studiosity... Microsoft Copilot... Adobe Express... Google Scholar Labs... While AI tools are permitted in OPEN assessment, you are responsible for the content of your assignment... Always check AI generated output carefully. Don't forget to acknowledge your use of AI.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The library guide frames listed tools as MQ-accessible learning supports and repeats responsibility, checking, and acknowledgement expectations.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: AI @ MQ and Quick guidelines for using AI @ MQ

artifacts.claimText: Macquarie Library referencing guidance says students who use generative AI in assessments should clearly state how they used it and fact-check AI responses against reliable sources.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If you use generative AI in your assessments, you should clearly state how you used it. Generative AI tools can produce inaccurate, biased, or outdated information and may collect personal data. Investigate each tool before using it and fact check all responses using reliable sources such as textbooks, peer reviewed articles, and reference works.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Students are told to disclose assessment AI use and verify AI outputs using reliable sources.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: How to cite materials generated by AI tools

artifacts.claimText: Macquarie's research guidance says researchers must not use generative AI for peer review activities, substantive research-output content including HDR theses, or critical components of human ethics, animal ethics, or biosafety applications.

citation.citationTitle: Using Generative Artificial Intelligence in Research
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using Generative Artificial Intelligence in Research
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Researchers must not use Generative AI: to perform peer review activities; to generate substantive content of research outputs, including HDR theses; for writing the critical components of human ethics, animal ethics, or biosafety applications.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The research guidance sets explicit prohibited research uses for GenAI.

artifacts.claimText: Macquarie's research guidance says researchers using generative AI should mitigate sensitive-data risks, keep oversight and control, critically review outputs, and disclose or acknowledge generative AI use in research.

citation.citationTitle: Using Generative Artificial Intelligence in Research
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using Generative Artificial Intelligence in Research
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Researchers must exercise care in the use of Generative AI in other aspects of their research and should... mitigate risks around the insecure storage or unauthorised re-use of sensitive data... exert oversight and control... carefully and critically review the output and results created by Generative AI... their use must be acknowledged and disclosed.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The research guidance requires careful controls around sensitive data, oversight, review, and disclosure of GenAI use.

artifacts.title: Macquarie University staged AI policy crawl draft
artifacts.summary: Staged draft summarising official Macquarie sources on the Responsible and Ethical Use of Artificial Intelligence Policy, Open/Observed assessment guidance, Academic Integrity Policy treatment of unauthorised generative AI use, Waranara Library student AI and referencing guidance, and research guidance for generative AI. This bundle remains staged agent-reviewed data and does not publish canonical public claims.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Claims are scoped to the cited official Macquarie source pages and PDF; assessment permission remains task-specific through Unit Guides and iLearn.
The TECHE teaching blog page was rejected because it disclaims being an official administration position.
The SharePoint research guidance page redirected to Microsoft sign-in and was not accessed or used as evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
